design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate action platformer levels with dual mode system (normal action levels vs. big mode levels), progressing through increasing difficulty
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual difficulty tracks allowing players to approach progression non-linearly; regional versions have different difficulty curves and starting points
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Schaefer from the Predator film, engaging in tactical platforming combat scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Regional design philosophy divergence—Japanese version emphasizes challenge (starts in Big Mode with harder design), while US/AU versions prioritize accessibility (starts in normal action level, made easier). This suggests deliberate tuning for regional player preferences. Hidden level select implies designers expected/encouraged sequence breaking.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Significant agency through hidden level skip mechanic (requires second controller input in Japanese Famicom version; documented as impossible on standard US NES hardware). Level select hidden in all versions suggests developer intention to let skilled players bypass difficulty gates.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Big Mode vs. Normal Mode level tracks; levels presented in different sequence across Japanese and international versions
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select code enables sequence breaking and challenge runs
Dual mode structure (normal action levels + big mode levels) allows multiple progression paths

world_building_techniques: Title screen choice between Schaefer and Predator imagery establishes dual protagonist presentation
Regional copyright/title screen variations suggest localization for narrative context

danger_signals: Increased difficulty in Japanese version signals regional design target differentiation
Big Mode designation implies escalating threat/challenge tier
environmental_storytelling: Level design progression from action platforming to 'big mode' suggests narrative escalation
Title screen imagery toggle between human protagonist and alien predator frames thematic conflict
